headline: Does AI Rebound and China Chip Access Shift Change The Bull Case For Lam Research (LRCX)?
summary: In recent days, Lam Research has been swept up in a broader rebound in semiconductor and AI infrastructure stocks, helped by reports that China may ease restrictions on advanced Nvidia AI chip imports and by strong institutional interest in AI-focused chip offerings such as SK Hynix’s oversubscribed US ADR. This sector-wide enthusiasm, reinforced by analysts tying Lam’s tools to the buildout of AI data center capacity and custom chips at hyperscalers like Meta, highlights how macro policy...

headline: Analysts Think AI Demand Has No Ceiling And Raised AMD’s Price Target Again
summary: Stifel just blew past Wall Street's AMD consensus with a target that would push the chipmaker into trillion-dollar territory, and the conditions required to get there are more specific than most investors realize.

headline: Chips Made In The USA Are Making A Comeback. What Investors Should Know.
summary: Semiconductor manufacturing was dominated by American companies before factories moved to countries like Taiwan and South Korea.  Now, hints of that original vigor are emerging again as a fab construction boom promises to revive what once was the global standard: computer chips made in the USA.  "Today, all of the major semiconductor manufacturers capable of building a state-of-the-art semiconductor are investing in the United States," said Joe Stockunas, president of the Americas unit of the SEMI industry trade group.
